title: FLP expanding into prosperity
primary_topics: FLP expansion to 'prosperity side' of philosophy
sub_topics: Esmel = Entrepreneurship / Sustainability / Management / Economics / business Law
Asian Institute of Management / ADMU / DLSU / UP / UST as eligible Esmel schools
FLP Legal Scholarship Program (6th year, Tan Yan Kee Foundation)
Office of the Bar Confidant pass-rate eligibility criterion

stances.claim: FLP is expanding its reach this school year to the 'prosperity side' of its philosophy via the Esmel Fellowships — bringing the foundation's twin-beacons formula into operational balance between liberty (legal scholarships) and prosperity (graduate management/economics fellowships).
Esmel grants are merit-based and 'could be awarded even to those already holding fellowships from other entities' — merit over need is FLP's consistent operating principle.

FLP's ultimate projects are the immersive interactive Museum for Liberty and Prosperity (liberty side) and the multibillion-peso Entrepreneurship Fund (prosperity side) — 'a program to help the poor help themselves through private entrepreneurship.'

signature_phrases: the prosperity side of its philosophy
to perpetuate its core judicial philosophy
to help the poor help themselves through private entrepreneurship
decision_framework_signals: applies twin-beacons framework to institutional-program design (liberty-side + prosperity-side complementarity)
uses merit-over-need eligibility principle as FLP operating doctrine
frames ultimate-projects (Museum + Entrepreneurship Fund) as physical-institutional manifestations of the twin-beacons philosophy
